original_reasoning: هرقل=Heraclius (Byzantine emperor) — direct borrowing of Heracles name into Arabic; same person/name
Subbukht (Arabic surname) — same Aramaic root as sabachthani (you have forsaken me); shared Semitic origin, same consonant cluster
ثفل and low-lying residue align well with litharge as metallic dross or sediment; the material-result sense is preserved.
الحضير is something collected into a mass; Greek lump is a close concrete counterpart.
Arabic explicitly includes 'name for the noble chief', and Greek Cleitarchus is transparently built on fame plus rule/leadership; this is a clear leadership match.
Arabic explicitly includes stammering or repetition in speech, while Greek means babble or prattle. This is a clear semantic chain with matching speech behavior.
حشو is stuffing or what is put inside, and λέχος is a bed/couch that takes filling and padding.
